examples.title: Confusion matrix
examples.description: A 3-class confusion matrix showing predicted vs actual classification results.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { MatrixChart } from '@lostgradient/cinder/matrix-chart';

  const data = [
    { actual: 'Cat', predicted: 'Cat', count: 50 },
    { actual: 'Cat', predicted: 'Dog', count: 5 },
    { actual: 'Cat', predicted: 'Bird', count: 2 },
    { actual: 'Dog', predicted: 'Cat', count: 3 },
    { actual: 'Dog', predicted: 'Dog', count: 42 },
    { actual: 'Dog', predicted: 'Bird', count: 1 },
    { actual: 'Bird', predicted: 'Cat', count: 4 },
    { actual: 'Bird', predicted: 'Dog', count: 2 },
    { actual: 'Bird', predicted: 'Bird', count: 38 },
  ];
</script>

<MatrixChart
  label="Confusion matrix — Animal classifier"
  description="Rows are actual classes; columns are predicted classes. Diagonal cells are correct predictions."
  {data}
  xField="predicted"
  yField="actual"
  valueField="count"
  colorScale="sequential"
  dataTableVisibility="visible"
/>

examples.title: Performance heatmap
examples.description: Team performance scores across quarters, colored with a diverging scale.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { MatrixChart } from '@lostgradient/cinder/matrix-chart';

  const data = [
    { team: 'Engineering', quarter: 'Q1', score: 82 },
    { team: 'Engineering', quarter: 'Q2', score: 91 },
    { team: 'Engineering', quarter: 'Q3', score: 78 },
    { team: 'Engineering', quarter: 'Q4', score: 95 },
    { team: 'Design', quarter: 'Q1', score: 74 },
    { team: 'Design', quarter: 'Q2', score: 68 },
    { team: 'Design', quarter: 'Q3', score: 85 },
    { team: 'Design', quarter: 'Q4', score: 90 },
    { team: 'Marketing', quarter: 'Q1', score: 60 },
    { team: 'Marketing', quarter: 'Q2', score: 72 },
    { team: 'Marketing', quarter: 'Q3', score: 65 },
    { team: 'Marketing', quarter: 'Q4', score: 88 },
    { team: 'Sales', quarter: 'Q1', score: 88 },
    { team: 'Sales', quarter: 'Q2', score: 93 },
    { team: 'Sales', quarter: 'Q3', score: 97 },
    { team: 'Sales', quarter: 'Q4', score: 84 },
  ];
</script>

<MatrixChart
  label="Team performance scores by quarter"
  description="Higher scores indicate stronger team performance for the period."
  {data}
  xField="quarter"
  yField="team"
  valueField="score"
  colorScale="diverging"
  dataTableVisibility="visible"
/>
